category_title: At-risk special education students who struggle to visualize historical narratives and connect emotionally to printed text
student_description: These are at-risk special education students who struggle with traditional reading and writing, often rushing through texts efferently without visualizing or emotionally connecting to the material. They experience severe anxiety and articulation difficulties with standard reports, but thrive when historical narratives are anchored in visual media and when assessments leverage their generational literacies (like texting or music).

criteria.rationale: Traditional tests and reports cause this student to freeze and fail to articulate their actual comprehension.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan includes non-traditional, creative assessment formats that leverage the student's generational skills (e.g., text-message dialogues, multimedia projects) instead of relying solely on standard essays.

criteria.rationale: The student grasps complex themes more efficiently when they can relate the curriculum to their own background experiences and modern interests.
criteria.check: The tutor incorporates ongoing activities that require the student to map historical or literary themes onto their own life, modern music, or personal interests.
